title: guards.Role reads $HOME directly and its C1 security regression test silently verifies the WRONG path when HOME is unset — plus always-safe allows never assert Alarm=false
summary: role.go:97 isAlwaysSafe reads os.Getenv("HOME") raw; role_test.go:12-17 homeDir() falls back to '/tmp' when HOME is unset, making TestRole_DeniesGlobalSettingsInCycle (role_integrity_test.go:94) test /tmp/.claude/settings.json — which is itself always-safe → the guard short-circuits to Allow before IsProtectedSurface, and the C1 regression (deny global settings edits mid-cycle, the most security-critical predicate in the codebase) passes for the wrong reason in HOME-less sandboxes/CI. Sibling gap: TestRole_AlwaysSafeDirs (role_test.go:148-163) asserts Allow but never !Alarm, so a regression that alarms on every /tmp write (flooding operator signal, masking real violations) is undetectable.
root_cause: Production env read without a seam + tests inheriting the machine's real HOME — the exact hermeticity wound class that produced past local-vs-CI splits (leak-recover git-identity lesson).
fix: Give isAlwaysSafe an injected home (param or os.UserHomeDir seam consistent with the package's existing style); every test exercising $HOME/.claude paths uses t.Setenv("HOME", t.TempDir()). Extend TestRole_AlwaysSafeDirs to assert !dec.Alarm on each allow. Add the unset-HOME case as an explicit test.
acceptance: RED: TestRole_C1_DeniesGlobalSettingsUnsetHome — with HOME unset/isolated, the settings.json deny+alarm still fires (today the test silently tests an always-safe path)
go vet, -race, apicover -enforce green; hermetic under env -u HOME
console_hold_note: OPERATOR HOLD 2026-07-22 (cycle-1041, 5th burn of the control-plane class this batch; prose fences proven non-binding 2x on one item): held in .evolve/inbox/console/ — INVISIBLE to wave triage (subdir, maxdepth-1 readers) until PR #349's plan-time gate deploys at the batch boundary. Reverse: mv back to .evolve/inbox/ after the new binary is live; the gate then enforces routing as plumbing.
